title: chrysobalanaceae
intro: The Chrysobalanaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the cocoa-nut family or the family of the sour soups. This family includes approximately 850 species of trees and shrubs, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Chrysobalanaceae plants are known for their distinctive, often large and showy flowers, and their edible fruits, such as the cupuacu and the surinam cherry. The name Chrysobalanaceae comes from the Greek words 'chrysos' meaning gold and 'balanos' meaning acorn, referring to the golden color of the flowers and the shape of the fruit.
